question: What is the configuration of the ECCS valves?

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications, and highlights corrosion protection measures such as weld cladding with stainless steel and nickel-based alloys.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain fuel integrity, minimize solid deposits, and ensure uniform boron concentration under all operating conditions, supporting both performance and safety requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section outlines the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It specifies that DHRS actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, and that comprehensive functional and position verification tests are conducted according to technical specifications, including a one-time in-situ test during initial startup.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity during both normal and emergency operations.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: The System Evaluation section describes how the NuScale Power Plant optimizes performance across various power levels by establishing steady-state parameters for both primary and secondary systems, such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures. These parameters are calculated using thermal hydraulic software and verified by hand, with uncertainties and design margins considered to ensure efficient operation and safety. The design supports natural circulation with low pressure losses, and uncertainties like SG tube plugging do not affect primary coolant flow determination.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various subsystems, and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and design requirements for threaded fasteners used in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the preservice and inservice inspection and testing procedures for pressure-retaining components, which must comply with the latest ASME BPVC Section XI standards as mandated by federal regulations.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases related to materials, quality assurance, and inspection procedures for pressurized water reactors.

edges.description: Section A provides detailed component and material information about the pressurizer, which is one of the RCS subsystems generally described in Section B.

Section B describes the function and operation of reactor vent valves (RVVs) as part of the ECCS, while Section A references the physical components and materials of the pressurizer and related nozzles, which are connected to the ECCS valve configuration described in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's design, while Section B specifies inspection and classification details for actuation valves (including ECCS valves) that are part of the system described in Section A.

Section B provides specific information about the function and operation of reactor vent valves (RVVs) as part of the ECCS, which are not detailed in the general RCS configuration described in Section A.

Section B describes the function and operation of reactor vent valves (RVVs) as part of the ECCS operation, while Section A discusses inspection and classification requirements for actuation valves (including those in the ECCS), thus Section A provides regulatory and inspection details for the valves whose operational role is described in Section B.

Section A references Section B for further description of the steam generators (SGs), indicating that details relevant to system configuration, such as ECCS valve connections, may be found in Section B.

Section A discusses system evaluation criteria for optimal performance, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor coolant system design, with both sections connected by their focus on the configuration and operation of reactor coolant system components relevant to ECCS valve configuration.
Section B provides specific details about the classification and inspection requirements of DHRS actuation valves, which are components relevant to the system performance discussed in Section A.

Section A describes operational steps involving ECCS valve preparation, while Section B provides material and component details for the pressurizer, which is referenced in Section A's operational context.

Section A describes specific operational steps involving ECCS valve preparation (such as opening RVVs and RRVs), while Section B provides the general design context of the reactor coolant system to which these valves belong.

Section A describes the operational steps involving ECCS valves (RVVs and RRVs) during system layup, while Section B specifies the inspection and classification requirements for actuation valves (including ECCS valves) as Category B, connecting operational configuration with regulatory classification.

Section B describes the sequence of operations and conditions required before opening the ECCS valves (RVVs and RRVs), which provides specific procedural context to the system evaluation discussed in Section A.

Section A describes the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B references the material descriptions for pressurizer components and points to Section 5.2 for further details, thus both sections are connected through their discussion of material specifications for RPV and pressurizer components.

Section A provides material and design details for threaded fasteners used in components like RPV and PZR flanges, while Section B gives a general overview of the RCS configuration, including the RPV and PZR, thus Section A specifies details for components generally described in Section B.

Section A discusses the material and inspection requirements for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B describes inspection requirements for DHRS actuation valves, both referencing ASME BPVC and inspection codes, thus connecting through shared regulatory standards for component integrity.
Section B references Section A for material descriptions of pressurizer components, indicating that details in Section A inform the component configuration discussed in Section B.

Section A lists references that may provide supporting standards or guidelines, while Section B gives a general description of the reactor coolant system design, which could be further detailed or specified by the referenced documents in Section A.

Section A provides references to regulatory and industry standards that underpin the design and quality assurance of reactor coolant boundary components, while Section B describes the classification and inspection requirements for ECCS-related valves, both being connected through their reliance on compliance with referenced standards for system configuration and integrity.
Section A discusses the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary and cites Section B as references for applicable industry guidelines and standards.
